content:
Another powerful symbol is the thorn bush, which gets caught on Phoenix’s dress as she walks through the woods. Instead of letting it stop her, she carefully frees herself and moves on. This moment represents the struggles she faces in life. She even says, “Thorns, you doing your appointed work. Never want to let folks pass, no sir” (Welty, 1941). This quote shows that she understands life is full of difficulties, but she refuses to let them hold her back. The thorn bush symbolizes all the obstacles that try to stop her, but her determination helps her keep moving forward.

By using the symbols of the worn path and the thorn bush, Welty highlights Phoenix’s strength and the sacrifices she makes for her grandson. Her journey is one of love and endurance, showing how powerful a person’s will can be. A Worn Path is a meaningful story about resilience and never giving up, even when the journey is hard.

References

Welty, E. (1941). A Worn Path. The Atlantic Monthly.

from Week 5 Discussion
